compiled_state.title: As a new project member, I see an orientation message and phase counter when onboarding starts so I know what to expect
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add an orientation message at the start of /dso:onboarding and a phase counter (Phase X of Y) that tracks progress through each major phase
**Why**: Non-technical users currently start onboarding with no context about what the process is, what it produces, or how long it takes
**Scope**:
- IN: Orientation message naming artifacts (project-understanding.md, dso-config.conf, shim), phase counter visible at start of each phase, dynamic Y that adjusts when phases are skipped (e.g., Phase 2 after template install), consider Task list tool for built-in UI progress
- OUT: Estimated time remaining, percentage-based progress bars

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, onboarding displays a message at startup explaining this is a one-time configuration and listing the artifacts it will produce
  ← Satisfies: "At the start of onboarding, the skill displays an orientation message"
- When this story is complete, a phase counter (Phase X of Y) appears at the start of each major phase, where Y reflects the skill's actual phase structure and adjusts when phases are skipped
  ← Satisfies: "A phase counter (Phase X of Y) is visible at the start of each major phase"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Phase counter must handle conditional phases (1.5, 1.6, 1.7) — dynamic Y that adjusts
- [Maintainability] Phase counter Y must be derived from skill phase structure, not hardcoded

comments.body: Escalation policy: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Consideration (adversarial review): Phase counter Y must account for the new pre-Phase 1 dependency scan added by 4c55-0c7a. Since both stories are independent, ensure Y is derived dynamically from the actual phase list, not hardcoded.
comments.author: Test
